industries.name: Electronics & Semiconductors
uses: Thallium bromide-iodide (TlBr, TlI, KRS-5: TlBr₀.₄₆I₀.₅₄) infrared-transmitting crystals for IR spectroscopy windows and optical elements — transmit from 0.5 to 40 μm, covering a wider IR range than germanium or ZnSe

Thallium-activated sodium iodide NaI(Tl) scintillator crystals: the universal gamma detection material used in radiation monitoring instruments, nuclear medicine gamma cameras, and survey meters — Tl doping provides the scintillation efficiency and emission wavelength matched to photomultiplier tubes

Thallium-doped cesium iodide CsI(Tl) scintillator for X-ray flat panel detectors in digital radiography — produces green light emission well-matched to photodiode sensitivity, the dominant flat-panel X-ray detector scintillator

High-temperature superconductors: thallium barium calcium copper oxide (Tl-BCCO, Tl₂Ba₂Ca₂Cu₃O₁₀) has one of the highest critical temperatures among cuprate superconductors (~127K) — research material for understanding superconducting mechanism

companies.name: Saint-Gobain Crystals
companies.use: World's largest producer of NaI(Tl) and CsI(Tl) scintillator crystals. Grows large-volume NaI(Tl) crystals for nuclear medicine gamma cameras, radiation portal monitors, and survey instruments. Major thallium consumer for crystal doping.

companies.name: Amcrys-H
companies.use: Major producer of NaI(Tl) and CsI(Tl) scintillator crystals for radiation detection, medical imaging, and industrial measurement applications. Supplies crystals to detector manufacturers globally.

industries.name: Medical Imaging
uses: Thallium-201 (²⁰¹Tl, t½ = 73 hours) cardiac SPECT imaging: thallium mimics potassium, concentrating in viable myocardial cells proportional to blood flow — used for myocardial perfusion imaging to detect coronary artery disease. Being replaced by Tc-99m sestamibi but still used at facilities without cyclotrons
²⁰¹Tl produced by proton bombardment of stable ²⁰³Tl in a cyclotron — thallium-201 is one of the few cyclotron-produced radioisotopes still in routine clinical use

Thallium stress testing: ²⁰¹Tl injected at peak exercise (or pharmacological stress) concentrates in perfused myocardium; redistribution images show ischemic but viable zones distinguishable from infarcted tissue

companies.name: Lantheus Holdings
companies.use: Major nuclear medicine imaging agent company. Produces ²⁰¹Tl chloride for cardiac SPECT myocardial perfusion imaging. Also produces ⁹⁹ᵐTc-based agents that are gradually displacing thallium in many centers.

industries.name: Specialty Glass & Research
uses: Thallium oxide in specialty optical glass formulations: increases refractive index without increasing dispersion in infrared-transmitting chalcogenide and heavy metal oxide glasses
Thallium formate and malonate solutions as dense liquids (density up to 4.3 g/cm³) for float separation of dense minerals in geological and ore beneficiation research

Thallium is highly toxic (comparable to lead and arsenic) — strict regulatory controls limit uses and require careful handling. Its toxicity historically led to use as rat poison and depilatory, now banned in most countries

companies.name: Sigma-Aldrich (Merck KGaA)
companies.use: Major supplier of thallium compounds (thallium nitrate, thallium acetate, thallium chloride) for research, analytical chemistry, and specialty glass preparation. Supplies controlled thallium reagents to qualified research and industrial customers.
